SHUBHANG SHARMA.
$\sum_i\varphi_i$
~ MAY 03.2026, HYDERABAD.

ACADEMIC
MATH-PH
FIELDNOTES
BLOG
CREATIVE

A break

Last weekend, I was already on a break from studying. I needed it. After trying to work a couple of days after that, I once again find myself pausing it. In the last two days, I took a break. I took a break to energise myself with some academic motivation. It’s very linear sometimes, just reading reading, checking off section by section, chapter by chapter, without any sense of a medium-term goal. There is a short term goal, and then there’s a long-term goal that I can tick off. Now that I know it’s going to take a lot of time into the Linear Algebra book, I feel a little unready to face the time.

Actually, this energising and missing some motivation in a grand scheme, is only a small part of the story. I think what happened two days ago was I pushed and used more energy than I can afford into reaching that next section and that next section of problems. Then I used my half-energy brain to solve some. Then I got stuck in that zone of dumbed-down brain mode and not able to solve problems creatively and think with all juices fired. Then that halted the progress… And oh boy, halted progress in this time gives such a negative feedback… So yeah, I needed change of mood, change of juices. Hopefully this blog marks a reset in moods, and I can start the usual study process after this night’s sleep (it’s 1:45 AM now).

Yeah, hopefully.

So last two days, I took time off to read some symplectic geometry! Have been wanting to read it since forever, and yeah took the time to just read a review article on it.

I will write a small technical blog on it, narrating ideas that I could register about Poission geometry, Symplectic Geometry, Classical Mechanics and some of the research directions involving them.

Such entertaining side-reads are always nice. I tend to lack some headspace when I am really into one task, but yeah since I am trying to slow down on the consumption side of things, perhaps I can try to side-read such stuff instead of pushing too much on that main-study (which seems to backfire often).

Let’s go Shubhang. Breathe. Rejuvenate. Let’s go. Hope. Hold onto it.

Oh I didn’t even talk about the things that made me feel good. So I was able to read through the article very nicely. Of course, there are lot of things that I need to learn rigorously. But it was a highly readable language! A lot of things that I have been studying in linear algebra over the last few days, were part of the language naturally and it was just a pleasant read. And along with that, of course a grad level course in differential geometry just smoothens the readability curve A LOT!

So yeah, I am in quite a sweet spot to venture into solid stuff. (Don’t get ahead of yourself xd, there’s lot to cover - But there’s confidence in being able to navigate the directions, and pick things up as I go…)

It’s very unproductive to not be in a natural relationship with the fundamental language of math involving analysis, algebra and topology. So this current preparation of mine primarily eliminates that gap and creates that natural relationship. All in hope to be able to work (research) in that language.

I will get busy studying then.

Art $\times$ Science
